overview: Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) is an apex body established through an Act of Parliament (ANRF Act, 2023) to provide high-level strategic direction for research, innovation, and entrepreneurship in natural sciences, engineering, technology, environmental and earth sciences, health and agriculture, and scientific and technological interfaces of humanities and social sciences. It promotes research and development across India’s universities, colleges, research institutions, and R&D laboratories, fostering collaborations among industry, academia, and government. ANRF aligns its strategies with Viksit Bharat 2047 and global best practices in research and development agencies worldwide.
objectives: Provide high-level strategic directions for research, innovation, and entrepreneurship
Promote research and development and foster a culture of research and innovation throughout India’s Universities, Colleges, Research Institutions, and R&D laboratories
Act as an apex body to provide high-level strategic direction of scientific research in the country as per recommendations of the National Education Policy
Forge collaborations among the industry, academia, and government departments and research institutions
Align strategies with the goals of Viksit Bharat 2047 and follow global best practices adopted by research and development agencies across the world
Support investigator-driven research across foundational, applied, and translational domains
Strengthen core scientific domains by supporting fundamental frontier research with potential for transformative long-term impact
Catalyse interdisciplinary research and cross-sectoral collaboration, fostering convergence across disciplines to address complex scientific questions
Promote early-stage academia–industry–research ecosystem collaboration to accelerate translation, technology development, and societal impact
Provide funding support to researchers belonging to Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe to undertake research in frontier areas of science and engineering
eligibility: Applicants (Principal Investigator and Co-Principal Investigator(s)) should be Indian citizens. Foreign nationals (including OCI and NRI) are also eligible to apply provided they fulfil the eligibility criteria notified. The applicant(s) must hold a Ph.D. degree in Sciences, Mathematics and Engineering. Applicants with Master’s Degree in medicine, surgery, or dental sciences (M.D. / M.S. / M.D.S. / M.V.Sc.) can apply two years after obtaining degree. Under Quantitative Social Sciences area, PI and Co-Investigator(s) should hold Ph.D. degree in Science, Mathematics, Engineering, M.D. / M.S. / M.D.S. / M.V.Sc. degree or Ph.D degree in any Social Sciences area. The applicant(s) (PIs and Co-PIs) must hold a regular position in a recognized academic institution or National Research Laboratory or in any other recognized research organization in India. A research professor or Professor of Practice may be a Co-PI. Private Academic institutions with valid UGC / AICTE / PCI approval, Private R&D Institutions with valid DSIR-SIRO recognition and Voluntary and Non-Governmental Organizations registered under NITI-AAYOG Darpan portal are eligible to host a project. NGOs must have a valid DARPAN portal registration, and DSIR SIRO recognition to receive direct grant-in-aid under the program. The term 'Regular' in the context of ARG refers to applicants who are appointed against a sanctioned post or on a tenure-track post of an Institute/University, and are likely to be renewed after the end of the tenure. Research Associates, Post-doctoral fellows, Ad-hoc faculties, Guest Faculties, Visiting Scientists, Consultants, Project Fellows, Faculty members/researchers whose contract is renewed every year are not eligible to apply. INSPIRE Faculty Fellowship, Ramanujan Fellowship, and Ramalingaswamy Fellowship holders are also eligible to apply as PI or Co-PI subject to fulfilling the service criteria as indicated below. Service Duration: In general, PI should have at least 'the duration of the proposed project plus one year' of official service remaining at the time of submission of proposals (for purposes of computation, the last date for submission of proposals will be taken). PI who will be superannuating during the duration of the project or already superannuated are also eligible to apply. In such cases, a Faculty/Scientist from the host institute who has at least the duration of the proposed project plus one year of official service remaining should be associated as a Co-PI. Under no condition will ANRF projects be allowed to continue with a PI or Co-PI aged 70. Such individual’s host institution should ensure the transfer of PI responsibilities to Co-PI. Investigators can hold a maximum of two ANRF (or erstwhile SERB) projects at any point of time. In one project, they may be a PI and in another Co-PI; or, they could be Co-PIs in two projects. If an individual has hit or crossed this limit, they may not apply in this cycle of ARG. This limitation, however, does not apply to PIs involved in IRG/EMEQ schemes or PIs / Co-PIs in mission-mode programs (ANRF MAHA, ATRI, PAIR programs or other similar Mission programs of ANRF). Investigators become eligible to apply for another project within six months from the date of completion of the ongoing project. Application for another project more than six months prior to the completion will not be considered. In respect of cases that are not mentioned above, ANRF reserves the right to determine eligibility.
benefits: Financial support for projects including funding for research personnel, consumables, travel, field work costs, contingencies, other costs (to cover Cloud Compute Credits, Software Licenses, Publication Costs, Analytical / Facility Access charges) and overheads, and non-recurring support for equipment, software purchased, long-term purchase of cloud or remote facility access. Operational flexibility is provided to utilize funds under Recurring head (i.e. all sub-heads of Recurring) subject to conditions. Travel: Budget of up to Rs. 5 Lakhs will be provided for a project to undertake travel related to the project. International travel for project-related work is also permitted within this travel head. Contingency: Contingency funds of up to Rs. 5 Lakhs will be provided for a project. They can also be utilized for registration charges for international conferences. Publication and patent filing charges: Expenditure towards open access publications in top journals of any discipline is permitted, as long as they are publications resulting from this project. Funds under the recurring head can also be utilized for patent filing charges. Equipment: The change of approved or sanctioned equipment is generally not permitted and may only be considered on a case-by-case basis, provided it is within the sanctioned budget for equipment/s. The PI must obtain prior approval from ANRF to change the approved or sanctioned equipment. Industry or International Collaborations: PIs are encouraged to bring Industry or International partners in proposals which are industry relevant (or in collaboration with top rated international research institutions). Optionally, PIs are encouraged to invite cost-sharing from industry or international partners. An LoI from industry or international partner can be added in full proposal stage. Industry, institutional or international co-funding is NOT mandatory, but is encouraged, and should be declared. Proposals Costing Rs. 1 Cr and above: Inclusion of Co-PI(s) is mandatory. If the Co-PI is from a different institution, an appropriate budget should be proposed and adequately justified. Inclusion of Co-PI (or PI) with justified role(s) from emerging academic research institutions is preferred. Emerging academic research institutions are defined as any State University OR lower NIRF ranked research active public or private academic institution, i.e. those institutions having > 50 NIRF overall ranking. Note: National Research Laboratories, R&D Organizations and Deemed Universities under any Ministries/Departments except Department of Higher Education are not considered as emerging academic research institutions. For IRG: Funding is provided up to Rs. 60 lakh plus overheads for a period of three years. The measures for ease of doing research include flexibility to utilize resources under recurring head; flexibility in manpower recruitment; enhanced travel, contingency and overhead charges; and support for expenses related to open access publications and patent filing charges. International travel for project-related activities is also permitted. For ITS: This scheme provides to & fro economic class air fare by the shortest route, airport tax and visa fees for attending the specific event. Registration fee as per actual or Rs. 50,000/- whichever is less will be provided to young scientist (Age< 35 on the date of start of event) in addition to the above support. The support is provided on reimbursement basis as per actual expenditure incurred by the applicant within the guidelines of the scheme.
financial_support: The ARG Program offers funding for projects for a maximum period of five years. For a project beyond three-year duration, support beyond the initial three-year period will be provided in a phased manner (i.e. 3+1 for 4 years and 3+1+1 for five years), contingent upon demonstrated research progress, impact and outcomes, in addition to publications and patents. ANRF will introduce an impact assessment scorecard with a real time dashboard per project and expect regular project updates post award. All data generated from project must be shared in appropriate depositories and managed. All publication pre-prints funded by the project must be shared in the public domain. Budget: The upper limit for project funding under the ARG Program is ₹ 5 Crores. The research grant will be provided under two budget heads: Recurring [Research personnel, Consumables, Travel, Field Work Costs, Contingencies, other costs (to cover Cloud Compute Credits, Software Licenses, Publication Costs, Analytical / Facility Access charges) and Overheads] Non-Recurring (Equipment, Software Purchased, Long-term purchase of cloud or remote facility access). No reallocation of funds will be permissible from Non-recurring to Recurring heads or vice-versa. Operational flexibility is provided to utilize funds under Recurring head (i.e. all sub-heads of Recurring) subject to the following conditions: Research Personnel: Operational flexibility is provided for engaging research personnel in the project. The norms for emoluments, and general terms and conditions of research personnel engaged in the project will be as per ANRF norms, informed through Office Memoranda viz., ANRF/OM/N/01/2024 and SB/S9/Z-06/2019 and updated from time to time. All personnel, including Research Personnel engaged under the project, for the full/part duration of the project, are to be treated as temporary employees and governed by administrative rules/service conditions (for leave, TA/DA etc.) of the host institute. They shall not to be treated as employees of the ANRF or Government of India nor permanent employees of the host institute. Travel: Budget of up to Rs. 5 Lakhs will be provided for a project to undertake travel related to the project. International travel for project-related work is also permitted within this travel head. Contingency: Contingency funds of up to Rs. 5 Lakhs will be provided for a project. They can also be utilized for registration charges for international conferences. The PI shall ensure that the total funds sanctioned under the recurring head is not exceeded in any case. Publication and patent filing charges: Expenditure towards open access publications in top journals of any discipline is permitted, as long as they are publications resulting from this project. Funds under the recurring head can also be utilized for patent filing charges. Equipment: The change of approved or sanctioned equipment is generally not permitted and may only be considered on a case-by-case basis, provided it is within the sanctioned budget for equipment/s. The PI must obtain prior approval from ANRF to change the approved or sanctioned equipment. [Refer ANRF OM ANRF/OM/N-01/2024 for details on overheads, travel and contingency. Industry or International Collaborations: PIs are encouraged to bring Industry or International partners in proposals which are industry relevant (or in collaboration with top rated international research institutions). Optionally, PIs are encouraged to invite cost-sharing from industry or international partners. An LoI from industry or international partner can be added in full proposal stage. Industry, institutional or international co-funding is NOT mandatory, but is encouraged, and should be declared. Proposals Costing Rs. 1 Cr and above: Inclusion of Co-PI(s) is mandatory If the Co-PI is from a different institution, an appropriate budget should be proposed and adequately justified Inclusion of Co-PI (or PI) with justified role(s) from emerging academic research institutions is preferred. Emerging academic research institutions are defined as any State University OR lower NIRF ranked research active public or private academic institution, i.e. those institutions having > 50 NIRF overall ranking. Note: National Research Laboratories, R&D Organizations and Deemed Universities under any Ministries/Departments except Department of Higher Education are not considered as emerging academic research institutions. For IRG: Funding is provided up to Rs. 60 lakh plus overheads for a period of three years. The measures for ease of doing research include flexibility to utilize resources under recurring head; flexibility in manpower recruitment; enhanced travel, contingency and overhead charges; and support for expenses related to open access publications and patent filing charges. International travel for project-related activities is also permitted. For ITS: This scheme provides to & fro economic class air fare by the shortest route, airport tax and visa fees for attending the specific event. Registration fee as per actual or Rs. 50,000/- whichever is less will be provided to young scientist (Age< 35 on the date of start of event) in addition to the above support. The support is provided on reimbursement basis as per actual expenditure incurred by the applicant within the guidelines of the scheme.
grant_amount_per_entity: ₹ 5 Crores (for ARG), ₹ 60 lakh plus overheads (for IRG), to & fro economy class air fare, airport tax, visa fees, and registration fee as per actual or Rs. 50,000/- whichever is less (for ITS)
application_process: 1. Visit 'www.anrfonline.in'. 2. Click on 'SIGN IN / REGISTER' section. 3. If not a registered user, click on 'NEW USER'. 4. Fill all the required details in the registration form. 5. Click on the 'Submit' button. 6. After successful submission, a 'Verification mail' will be sent to the given mail id. 7. Click on the verification link to complete registration. 8. After login, go to Menu --> Proposal Submission --> Form Submission --> Select scheme and start filling the form online. 9. Submit the proposal through the website 'www.anrfonline.in'. 10. A pre-proposal must be submitted in the prescribed format by the deadline. 11. Applicants whose pre-proposals are shortlisted will be invited to submit a full proposal. 12. These full proposals will undergo a rigorous review by subject matter experts. 13. PIs may also be called for a presentation, whenever required. 14. Larger value proposals (exceeding ₹1 Cr) will undergo another round of evaluation at level III by the Empowered Technical Committee, with specific emphasis on impact, and depth of collaboration.
required_documents: Endorsement letter duly signed and stamped by competent authority of the institute
Copy of letter of acceptance of the presenting paper (oral/poster) by the organizers
Copy of the abstract of the paper to be presented
Biodata with complete list of Scientific / Technical publications and Patents, if any
Bio-data of the Applicant
Certificate from Principal Investigator
Endorsement Letter from the Head of Institution
Category Certificate
Plagiarism undertaking
Audited / interim Utilization Certificate (UC)
Statement of Expenditure (SE) for the financial year in the correct format
Annual / interim progress report, as per approved format
List of publications / patents, if any, from the project
List of Assets acquired till that Financial Year
Final Consolidated Statement of Expenditure (SE), giving expenditure financial year wise (FY) from date of start till date of completion
Utilization Certificates (FY wise), matching with Statement of Expenditure figures
Project Completion Report (PCR) with a list of publications, and patents filed/granted
DD/ cheque for the unspent funds, drawn in favour of 'Science and Engineering Research Fund', payable at New Delhi
Claim Form (to be generated from the online system while submitting claim details)
Bank Account Details
Air/Rail/Bus/Others tickets
Cash Receipt of Air Ticket
Original Boarding Passes
Visa Charges Receipt
Registration Receipt
Participation Certificate
Certificate(s) for amount received from other sources, if any
Best Available Fare Certificate (from authorized agent only)
Miscellaneous Upload
deadlines: Rolling basis — calls for proposals are notified through the website 'www.anrfonline.in'. Specific programs have defined windows: e.g., Call for nominations under Ramanujan Fellowships is open from 1 January 2026 to 31 December 2026 (5:00 pm); Call for Pre-Proposals under MAHA WATER Program is open from 01-June-2026 to 10-July-2026 (5:00 PM); International Travel Support (ITS) applications are accepted only for events whose start date is between 13 August, 2026 and 12 September, 2026 including both these dates (as of 14 June, 2026).
contact_details: Dr. K.K Magesh Kumar, Scientist 'E', Anusandhan National Research Foundation, 3rd & 4th Floor, Block II, Technology Bhavan, New Mehrauli Road, New Delhi-110016, Telephone: 011-26552114, 26552151, Email: ms[dot]its[at]anrf[dot]gov[dot]in; Dr. A.V. Balachandar (Scientist F), 011-26511091, venkatbala[at]serb[dot]gov[dot]in
caveats: No reallocation of funds will be permissible from Non-recurring to Recurring heads or vice-versa
Investigators can hold a maximum of two ANRF (or erstwhile SERB) projects at any point of time
Under no condition will ANRF projects be allowed to continue with a PI or Co-PI aged 70
Co-investigators are not allowed in the IRG scheme
An investigator will be allowed to avail IRG project for a maximum of three times in their career
Researchers/scientists who have ongoing projects under the IRG / erstwhile EMEQ scheme as of the date of submission of the proposal are not eligible to apply
The support given for the specific event under ITS cannot be used to attend another event
If the applicant to whom the travel grant has been awarded leaves the institution, the grant released, if any, should be refunded back by the Institute to ANRF

If any candidate found to have furnished incorrect / misleading information at any stage, his/her candidature will be cancelled and no reimbursement will be made. The candidate will also be debarred for next three years for availing support under this scheme
ANRF’s decisions are final and binding, and no requests for review or reconsideration will be entertained
Overlaps in the proposals submitted to different funding organizations or programs within Government of India (GoI) will be discouraged and not considered for support
